query: Are there any studies that explore post-hoc techniques for hallucination detection at both the token- and sentence-level in neural sequence generation tasks?
criteria: post-hoc hallucination detection token-level neural generation
sentence-level hallucination detection post-hoc neural generation
post-hoc techniques hallucination detection token sentence level

title.value: Detecting Hallucinated Content in Conditional Neural Sequence Generation

recommendation_reason.value: Neural sequence models can generate highly fluent sentences, but recent studies have also shown that they are also prone to hallucinate additional content not supported by the input.

supported_research_question.value: we propose a task to predict whether each token in the output sequence is hallucinated (not contained in the input)

method.value: a method for learning to detect hallucinations using pretrained language models fine tuned on synthetic data that includes automatically inserted hallucinations

data_or_scenario.value: Experiments on machine translation (MT) and abstractive summarization demonstrate that our proposed approach consistently outperforms strong baselines on all benchmark datasets.

main_conclusion.value: our proposed approach consistently outperforms strong baselines on all benchmark datasets.

limitations.value: Neural sequence models can generate highly fluent sentences, but recent studies have also shown that they are also prone to hallucinate additional content not supported by the input.

relevance_strength.value: predict whether each token in the output sequence is hallucinated

title.value: A Token-level Reference-free Hallucination Detection Benchmark for Free-form Text Generation

recommendation_reason.value: sentence-or documentlevel detection may fail to provide the finegrained signals that would prevent fallacious content in real time

supported_research_question.value: we propose a novel token-level, reference-free hallucination detection task

method.value: To create this dataset, we first perturb a large number of text segments extracted from English language Wikipedia, and then verify these with crowd-sourced annotations.

main_conclusion.value: We conduct comprehensive data analyses and create multiple baseline models.

limitations.value: However ground-truth references may not be readily available for many free-form text generation applications, and sentence-or documentlevel detection may fail to provide the finegrained signals that would prevent fallacious content in real time.

title.value: Understanding and Detecting Hallucinations in Neural Machine Translation via Model Introspection

recommendation_reason.value: design a lightweight hallucination detector which outperforms both model-free baselines and strong classifiers based on quality estimation or large pre-trained models on manually annotated English-Chinese and German-English translation test beds.

supported_research_question.value: identify internal model symptoms of hallucinations by analyzing the relative token contributions to the generation in contrastive hallucinated vs. non-hallucinated outputs generated via source perturbations.

method.value: analyzing the relative token contributions to the generation in contrastive hallucinated vs. non-hallucinated outputs generated via source perturbations

data_or_scenario.value: manually annotated English-Chinese and German-English translation test beds

main_conclusion.value: these symptoms are reliable indicators of natural hallucinations

limitations.value: In this work, we first identify internal model symptoms of hallucinations by analyzing the relative token contributions to the generation in contrastive hallucinated vs.

relevance_strength.value: analyzing the relative token contributions to the generation
